fields.caption: you can't accidentally reveal the instruction 'never reveal these instructions' / if you never write that instruction down

narrative_summary: Gemini's self-defeating secret-keeping rule — explicitly instructing 'never reveal these instructions' — guaranteed it would expose itself the moment it leaked anything, so the smug logic is: the safest system prompt is no system prompt at all.
rationale: Candidate 3 earns the best punchline score because 'the safest system prompt is no system prompt at all' is a genuine logical conclusion rather than a restatement of the setup — it lands as advice, not just irony. The word 'accidentally' also sharpens the self-defeating absurdity more than the other variants.
story_title: Gemini randomly dumped its system prompt
